title: Living in Boulder vlog 1 -  21 - 2018: Google Earthing Roman Colosseum Stairs

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on January 21, 2018, giving a progress update on his ongoing Google Earth reconstruction of the Roman Colosseum. He explains the stair and seating tier structure he has been mapping (lower rows, upper rows, tunnels, levels), describes his poor internet connection preventing him from loading Google's own 3D Rome model, and outlines his next steps: finishing the stairs, tracing the stands, color-coding zones, and eventually modeling the two-level basement.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Worth building his own because his internet is too slow to use Google's Rome and his version will be more understandable via organized folders
The stair routing is not totally simple and needs careful modeling so viewers can understand the design
